items.verse: وَإِن تَدْعُوهُمْ إِلَى ٱلْهُدَىٰ لَا يَسْمَعُوا۟ ۖ وَتَرَىٰهُمْ يَنظُرُونَ إِلَيْكَ وَهُمْ لَا يُبْصِرُونَ
items.verseEn: And if you invite them to guidance, they do not hear; and you see them looking at you while they do not see.

items.verse: وَمَثَلُ ٱلَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا۟ كَمَثَلِ ٱلَّذِى يَنْعِقُ بِمَا لَا يَسْمَعُ إِلَّا دُعَآءًۭ وَنِدَآءًۭ ۚ صُمٌّۢ بُكْمٌ عُمْىٌۭ فَهُمْ لَا يَعْقِلُونَ
items.verseEn: The example of those who disbelieve is like that of one who shouts at what hears nothing but calls and cries [i.e., cattle or sheep] - deaf, dumb and blind, so they do not understand.

items.verse: أَلَآ إِنَّهُمْ هُمُ ٱلْمُفْسِدُونَ وَلَـٰكِن لَّا يَشْعُرُونَ
items.verseEn: Unquestionably, it is they who are the corrupters, but they perceive [it] not.

items.verse: وَهُوَ ٱلَّذِى مَدَّ ٱلْأَرْضَ وَجَعَلَ فِيهَا رَوَٰسِىَ وَأَنْهَـٰرًۭا ۖ وَمِن كُلِّ ٱلثَّمَرَٰتِ جَعَلَ فِيهَا زَوْجَيْنِ ٱثْنَيْنِ ۖ يُغْشِى ٱلَّيْلَ ٱلنَّهَارَ ۚ إِنَّ فِى ذَٰلِكَ لَـَٔايَـٰتٍۢ لِّقَوْمٍۢ يَتَفَكَّرُونَ
items.verseEn: And it is He who spread the earth and placed therein firmly set mountains and rivers; and from all of the fruits He made therein two mates; He causes the night to cover the day. Indeed in that are signs for a people who give thought.

categories.name: أَنْوَاعٌ مِنَ النَّبَاتِ مَذْكُورَةٌ فِي الْقُرْآنِ
categories.nameEn: Specific types of non-fruit plants and trees mentioned in the Quran

items.verse: فَأَعْرَضُوا۟ فَأَرْسَلْنَا عَلَيْهِمْ سَيْلَ ٱلْعَرِمِ وَبَدَّلْنَـٰهُم بِجَنَّتَيْهِمْ جَنَّتَيْنِ ذَوَاتَىْ أُكُلٍ خَمْطٍۢ وَأَثْلٍۢ وَشَىْءٍۢ مِّن سِدْرٍۢ قَلِيلٍۢ
items.verseEn: But they turned away [refusing], so We sent upon them the flood of the dam, and We replaced their two [fields of] gardens with gardens of bitter fruit, tamarisks and something of sparse lote trees.
[They will be] among lote trees with thorns removed.
And We caused to grow over him a gourd vine.

items.verse: وَإِذْ قُلْتُمْ يَـٰمُوسَىٰ لَن نَّصْبِرَ عَلَىٰ طَعَامٍۢ وَٰحِدٍۢ فَٱدْعُ لَنَا رَبَّكَ يُخْرِجْ لَنَا مِمَّا تُنۢبِتُ ٱلْأَرْضُ مِنۢ بَقْلِهَا وَقِثَّآئِهَا وَفُومِهَا وَعَدَسِهَا وَبَصَلِهَا ۖ قَالَ أَتَسْتَبْدِلُونَ ٱلَّذِى هُوَ أَدْنَىٰ بِٱلَّذِى هُوَ خَيْرٌ ۚ ٱهْبِطُوا۟ مِصْرًۭا فَإِنَّ لَكُم مَّا سَأَلْتُمْ ۗ وَضُرِبَتْ عَلَيْهِمُ ٱلذِّلَّةُ وَٱلْمَسْكَنَةُ وَبَآءُو بِغَضَبٍۢ مِّنَ ٱللَّهِ ۗ ذَٰلِكَ بِأَنَّهُمْ كَانُوا۟ يَكْفُرُونَ بِـَٔايَـٰتِ ٱللَّهِ وَيَقْتُلُونَ ٱلنَّبِيِّـۧنَ بِغَيْرِ ٱلْحَقِّ ۗ ذَٰلِكَ بِمَا عَصَوا۟ وَّكَانُوا۟ يَعْتَدُونَ
items.verseEn: And [recall] when you said, "O Moses, we can never endure one [kind of] food. So call upon your Lord to bring forth for us from the earth its green herbs and its cucumbers and its garlic and its lentils and its onions." [Moses] said, "Would you exchange what is better for what is less? Go into [any] settlement and indeed, you will have what you have asked." And they were covered with humiliation and poverty and returned with anger from Allāh [upon them]. That was because they [repeatedly] disbelieved in the signs of Allāh and killed the prophets without right. That was because they disobeyed and were [habitually] transgressing.

categories.name: مُصْطَلَحَاتٌ قُرْآنِيَّةٌ لِأَجْرَامٍ سَمَاوِيَّةٍ وَتَرْتِيبَاتِهَا
categories.nameEn: Specific Quranic terms for celestial bodies or their arrangements

items.verseEn: So I swear by the retreating stars -
Those that run [their courses] and disappear [i.e., set]

items.verse: وَلَقَدْ جَعَلْنَا فِى ٱلسَّمَآءِ بُرُوجًۭا وَزَيَّنَّـٰهَا لِلنَّـٰظِرِينَ
items.verseEn: And We have placed within the heaven great stars and have beautified it for the observers.
Then I swear by the setting of the stars,

items.verse: أَفَلَا يَنظُرُونَ إِلَى ٱلْإِبِلِ كَيْفَ خُلِقَتْ
items.verseEn: Then do they not look at the camels - how they are created?

items.verse: فَتَقَبَّلَهَا رَبُّهَا بِقَبُولٍ حَسَنٍۢ وَأَنۢبَتَهَا نَبَاتًا حَسَنًۭا وَكَفَّلَهَا زَكَرِيَّا ۖ كُلَّمَا دَخَلَ عَلَيْهَا زَكَرِيَّا ٱلْمِحْرَابَ وَجَدَ عِندَهَا رِزْقًۭا ۖ قَالَ يَـٰمَرْيَمُ أَنَّىٰ لَكِ هَـٰذَا ۖ قَالَتْ هُوَ مِنْ عِندِ ٱللَّهِ ۖ إِنَّ ٱللَّهَ يَرْزُقُ مَن يَشَآءُ بِغَيْرِ حِسَابٍ
items.verseEn: So her Lord accepted her with good acceptance and caused her to grow in a good manner and put her in the care of Zechariah. Every time Zechariah entered upon her in the prayer chamber, he found with her provision. He said, "O Mary, from where is this [coming] to you?" She said, "It is from Allāh. Indeed, Allāh provides for whom He wills without account."

items.verse: يَـٰٓأَيُّهَا ٱلَّذِينَ ءَامَنُوا۟ لِمَ تَقُولُونَ مَا لَا تَفْعَلُونَ
items.verseEn: O you who have believed, why do you say what you do not do?

harf.hint: To open or expand, especially one's chest for guidance.
harf.arabicVerse: أَفَمَن شَرَحَ ٱللَّهُ صَدْرَهُۥ لِلْإِسْلَـٰمِ فَهُوَ عَلَىٰ نُورٍۢ مِّن رَّبِّهِۦ ۚ فَوَيْلٌۭ لِّلْقَـٰسِيَةِ قُلُوبُهُم مِّن ذِكْرِ ٱللَّهِ ۚ أُو۟لَـٰٓئِكَ فِى ضَلَـٰلٍۢ مُّبِينٍ
harf.verse: 39:22 — So is one whose breast Allāh has expanded to [accept] Islām and he is upon [i.e., guided by] a light from his Lord [like one whose heart rejects it]? Then woe to those whose hearts are hardened against the remembrance of Allāh. Those are in manifest error.

deduction.title: The Weight of Honesty
deduction.intro: In a community defined by faith and sacrifice, a moment of decision arrived. For some, the comfort of the present outweighed the call to duty. This story is about a small group whose choice led not to outright rebellion, but to a heavy-hearted absence. Their subsequent path was one of profound isolation and a test of truthfulness, where the entire world seemed to shrink around them, waiting for a verdict from the heavens.
clues: We were devoted followers of the final Prophet (ﷺ) in the city of Madinah.
When the call came for a difficult and distant expedition, we stayed behind, not from disbelief, but from procrastination and attachment to our comforts.
While others made false excuses to the Prophet (ﷺ), we chose the difficult path of telling the complete truth about our failure.
As a consequence of our actions, a severe command was issued: the entire community was forbidden from speaking to us.
Our trial reached its peak when we were even commanded to separate from our spouses, leaving us utterly isolated.
After fifty days of sincere repentance, our forgiveness was announced directly from Allah in verses that are recited to this day in Surah At-Tawbah.

identity.label: Who Are We?
options: The Hypocrites who made false excuses
The Companions of the Trench
The Ansar who pledged at 'Aqabah
The Three Who Were Left Behind
The Emigrants from Makkah (Muhajirun)
identity.answer: The Three Who Were Left Behind

trial.label: Trial/Event
options: Fleeing during the Battle of Uhud
Staying behind from the Expedition to Tabuk
Arguing over the spoils of the Battle of Badr
Spreading the slander against 'Aisha (ra)
Refusing to pay Zakat after the Prophet's death
trial.answer: Staying behind from the Expedition to Tabuk

location.label: Key Element
options: Imprisonment in a well
A 50-day social boycott
Being swallowed by a large fish
Building a rival mosque (Masjid al-Dirar)
Transformation into apes

outcome.label: Outcome
options: Receiving worldly leadership and a kingdom
Being exiled from the city of Madinah
Forgiveness revealed in the Quran
Being drowned by a flood
Losing all wealth and family

deduction.arabic: وَعَلَى ٱلثَّلَـٰثَةِ ٱلَّذِينَ خُلِّفُوا۟ حَتَّىٰٓ إِذَا ضَاقَتْ عَلَيْهِمُ ٱلْأَرْضُ بِمَا رَحُبَتْ وَضَاقَتْ عَلَيْهِمْ أَنفُسُهُمْ وَظَنُّوٓا۟ أَن لَّا مَلْجَأَ مِنَ ٱللَّهِ إِلَّآ إِلَيْهِ ثُمَّ تَابَ عَلَيْهِمْ لِيَتُوبُوٓا۟ ۚ إِنَّ ٱللَّهَ هُوَ ٱلتَّوَّابُ ٱلرَّحِيمُ
deduction.verse: And [He also forgave] the three who were left alone [i.e., boycotted, regretting their error] to the point that the earth closed in on them in spite of its vastness and their souls confined [i.e., anguished] them and they were certain that there is no refuge from Allāh except in Him. Then He turned to them so they could repent. Indeed, Allāh is the Accepting of Repentance, the Merciful. (9:118)

scramble.hint: A divine promise of future contentment and reward for the Prophet (ﷺ).
scramble.verseEn: And your Lord is going to give you, and you will be satisfied.

verse.arabic_text: وَقَالَ رَبُّكُمُ ٱدْعُونِىٓ أَسْتَجِبْ لَكُمْ ۚ إِنَّ ٱلَّذِينَ يَسْتَكْبِرُونَ عَنْ عِبَادَتِى سَيَدْخُلُونَ جَهَنَّمَ دَاخِرِينَ
verse.translation: And your Lord says, 'Call upon Me; I will respond to you.' Indeed, those who disdain My worship will enter Hell [rendered] contemptible.

theme_question.correct: The direct command to make supplication (Dua) with a guarantee of response.
options: The obligation of performing the five daily prayers.
The direct command to make supplication (Dua) with a guarantee of response.
The importance of giving charity to the poor.
The prohibition of associating partners with Allah.

educational_notes.verse_context: This verse comes in Surah Ghafir, also known as Al-Mu'min (The Believer). It is a powerful declaration from Allah Himself, inviting His servants to call upon Him directly. It follows discussions about the signs of Allah's power in creation, highlighting that the Creator is the only One worthy of being called upon.
educational_notes.theme_explanation: The verse establishes a direct and personal connection between the Creator and the creation. The act of 'Dua' (supplication) is presented not just as a request but as an act of worship ('ibadah). The promise 'I will respond to you' is a profound source of hope and comfort for believers. Conversely, refusing to make Dua is portrayed as an act of arrogance (yastakbirun), as it implies a self-sufficiency and a denial of one's need for Allah, which carries a severe warning.
educational_notes.surah_overview: Surah Ghafir (The Forgiver) is the 40th chapter of the Quran. It is a Meccan surah that begins with the Muqatta'at (disjointed letters) Ha, Mim. Its name comes from the mention of Allah as the 'Forgiver of sin' (Ghafir adh-Dhanb) in verse 3. A significant portion of the surah recounts the story of a believing man from the court of Pharaoh who concealed his faith and defended Prophet Musa (Moses), hence the surah's other name, Al-Mu'min (The Believer). The surah emphasizes the Oneness of Allah, the reality of revelation, and the consequences of arrogance and disbelief, contrasting them with the rewards for faith and humility.
